channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Driving digital transformation at the Saudi Accreditation Center: A conversation with Dr. Fahad Almoqhim, CIO
item.description: The Saudi Accreditation Center (SAAC) is crucial in advancing the Kingdom’s quality infrastructure, ensuring that businesses and organizations comply with rigorous standards. At the helm of the Center’s technological evolution is Dr.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: AI as a catalyst for ESG: Empowering CIOs to drive sustainable innovation
item.description: In today’s fast-evolving business landscape, environmental, social and governance (ESG) criteria have become fundamental to corporate responsibility and long-term success.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Login.gov facial recognition option gets GSA approval
item.description: Login.gov will allow its users to match a “selfie” with the photo on a government ID, such as a driver’s license. The post Login.gov facial recognition option gets GSA approval first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jory Heckman, Federal News Network

item.title: Google could be forced to sell parts of its business if the DOJ gets its way
item.description: The U.S. Department of Justice is considering asking a federal judge to force Google to sell parts of its business in order to eliminate its online search monopoly.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Climate solution: Form Energy secures $405M to speed development of long-awaited 100-hour battery
item.description: Form Energy, a company that is beginning to produce a longer-lasting alternative to lithium batteries, hit a milestone Wednesday with an announcement of $405 million in funding.
item.dc:creator: Isabella O'Malley, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Big Tech’s experimental AI can’t beat old-school productivity gems
item.description: The “Chat” in ChatGPT’s name neatly encapsulates a major element of why OpenAI’s AI-infused assistant has had such epoch-shifting impact. We’ve been using PCs and phones to chat with humans for years—at least since the era of AOL Instant Messenger.
item.dc:creator: Harry McCracken, Fast Company

item.title: Volunteers at Concordia Cemetery use technology to unearth Buffalo history
item.description: "We're going to be looking for and bringing up some of the tombstones that have sunk or have been buried," said Bonnie Fleischauer, Concordia Cemetery's public information officer.
item.dc:creator: Kayleigh Hunter-Gasperini, NewsNation

item.title: How to use your iPhone to get help by satellite
item.description: Apple’s decision to put satellite connections in iPhones is already saving lives, and as Hurricane Milton threatens Florida, now is the time for every US iPhone user to learn how the system works and why they should upgrade to iOS 18.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Jupiter’s moon Europa is NASA’s next search for potential life
item.description: Where there’s water, there’s life. At least on Earth. So, do the same rules apply off-world? We sent the Perseverance rover to an ancient Martian lakebed to gather clues of past life.
item.dc:creator: Susan Karlin, Fast Company

item.title: Toyota to invest additional $500 million in Bay Area-based electric aircraft maker Joby Aviation
item.description: The aviation company has designed and produced an electric air taxi that will carry a pilot and four passengers at speeds up to 200 mph offering high-speed mobility with a fraction of the noise produced by helicopters and zero operating emissions.
item.dc:creator: James Herrera, The Mercury News

item.title: Federal prosecutors eye asking judge to break up Google
item.description: The Department of Justice (DOJ) is considering asking a federal judge to order Google to break off its search business from other portions of the company to address its illegal monopoly over online search.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: US considers asking court to break up Google as it weighs remedies in the antitrust case
item.description: The U.S. Department of Justice is considering asking a federal judge to break up Google after its ubiquitous search engine was declared an illegal monopoly, but it is just one of many possible remedies under review, according to a court filing.
item.dc:creator: SHAWN CHEN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Weighing the risks of moving too fast with gen AI
item.description: When it comes to AI, the fear of missing out is real. According to research by Coleman Parkes Research on behalf of Riverbed, 91% of decision-makers at large companies are concerned their competitors will have an advantage if they get ahead with AI.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: TikTok is designed to be addictive to kids and causes them harm, US states' lawsuits say
item.description: More than a dozen states and the District of Columbia filed lawsuits against TikTok on Tuesday, saying that the popular short-form video app is designed to be addictive to kids and harms their mental health.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Federal Executive Forum Customer Experience Strategies in Government Progress and Best Practices 2024
item.description: Improving customer experience (CX) is a multi-faceted challenge to tackle as needs vary between stakeholders.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: Google Workspace vs. Microsoft 365: What’s the best office suite for business?
item.description: Once upon a time, Microsoft Office ruled the business world. By the late ’90s and early 2000s, Microsoft’s office suite had brushed aside rivals such as WordPerfect Office and Lotus SmartSuite, and there was no competition on the horizon.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: SAP Unveils Comprehensive AI-Powered Platform for Developers - A Game-Changer in Business Technology?
item.description: SAP's latest innovations promise to revolutionize the way developers work, with a focus on low-code, generative AI, and seamless integration between applications.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: DoD wringing out zero trust concepts under assessment process
item.description: The Pentagon is also working with major cloud service providers like Microsoft and Google to evaluate their zero trust implementations. The post DoD wringing out zero trust concepts under assessment process first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Musk to make more Pennsylvania appearances ahead of election
item.description: Billionaire tech mogul Elon Musk plans to make more appearances in Pennsylvania to boost former President Trump’s campaign in the final four weeks before the election.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: The AI Market: Open Source and the Concentration of Power
item.description: The intersection of open source and artificial intelligence, exploring the concentration of power in the market
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: 5 ways data scientists can prepare now for genAI transformation
item.description: Until recently, data scientists and analysts’ primary deliverables were data visualizations, machine learning models, dashboards, reports, and analytical insights used for storytelling.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: 5 ways companies can use time series forecasting
item.description: Time series forecasting is a powerful machine learning method that leverages historical time-stamped data to predict future events and help reduce uncertainty from business conditions — for example, to accurately predict sales, inventory levels, and even manufacturing data.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: ChatGPT o1-preview excels at code generation
item.description: “Ho, hum,” I thought in response to the release of the OpenAI o1 models. “Yet another stochastic parrot.” I was wrong. In fact, I tried the o1-preview model on one of my standard programming tasks, and found it to be surprisingly good.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: What to stream: Coldplay, an unhinged Charlie Puth, 'Salem’s Lot,' Sarah Paulson and NHL 25
item.description: Coldplay's 10th studio album and the American Music Awards celebrating their 50th anniversary are some of the new television, films, music and games headed to a device near you.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: How data brokers are fueling elder fraud in America
item.description: Criminals are increasingly turning to data brokers to provide access to private information that can be used to improve their scamming schemes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
